name: Up Helly Aa
traditions: Torchlit procession of guizers in Viking costumes; burning of a replica Viking longship; squad performances; all-night celebrations.
historicalBackground: Up Helly Aa began in the 1880s, replacing earlier rowdy celebrations. It honors Shetland's Norse heritage and the Viking influence on the islands.
description: Shetland's spectacular fire festival honoring Norse heritage with a dramatic torchlit procession and the burning of a Viking longship.
